metadata.name: Sanitized
metadata.description: This is the new diagnostics experience to help you drill down memory utilization on each instance serving your app and to identify the app and the corresponding process causing high memory issues (v20260214)

rows: No workers have been assigned to your app.

innerRendering.title: Memory usage for all hosts running this app was at or below 70%.  Expand to see top 10 memory% found.
innerRendering.description: The list is sorted by host's total site metrics (total Memory usage of all USER processes of all sites), followed by host and time.  The values for Mem% column below are based on data from the RSS column of the ps command and collected every 5 minutes.  The pct is computed by dividing the RSS value per process by the total memory reported by the top command.  .

renderingProperties.title: Memory usage for all hosts running this app was at or below 70%.  Expand to see top 10 memory% found.

innerRendering.title: Memory usage for all apps was at or below 60%.  Expand to see top 10 memory% found.
innerRendering.description: The list is sorted by app metrics (total Memory usage of an app's USER processes), followed by host and then time. High app metrics in this table is reflected in the icons of the Instance and Site dropdowns. The values for Mem% column below are based on data from the RSS column of the ps command and collected every 5 minutes. The pct is computed by dividing the RSS value per process by the total memory reported by the top command. .

renderingProperties.title: Memory usage for all apps was at or below 60%.  Expand to see top 10 memory% found.

innerRendering.title: Memory usage for Sanitized-webapp app was at or below 60%.  Expand to see top 10 memory% found.
innerRendering.description: The list is sorted by Sanitized-webapp app (any slot) metrics (total Memory usage of site's app's USER processes), followed by host, and time.  The values for Mem% column below are based on data from the RSS column of the ps command and collected every 5 minutes.  The pct is computed by dividing the RSS value per process by the total memory reported by the top command.  .

renderingProperties.title: Memory usage for Sanitized-webapp app was at or below 60%.  Expand to see top 10 memory% found.

The source of the memory usage data for most sites is the site's cgroup memory.usage_in_bytes value minus the inactive_file value which is returned from the underlying container runtime's performance API. Data sample is collected every 5m and reflects the average over the previous 5m.","False","null"],["Success","Instance Graphs Description","User/System Processes Graphs","These graphs show the sum of the memory usage percentages of all the user/system processes on the host. A user process is considered to be any process with a numeric userid. Non-numeric userids (such as 'root') are considered system processes. Each type of graph (user or system) is shown using data from the ps command which is less granular.

The user process graph(s) includes processes from all apps on all the sites in the App Service Plan on this system. Further down in the 'User Processes Memory Usage Per Site' section, you can view these processes per site.

The system process (top) graph includes the \"App Service Platform processes\" graph which includes all the essential processes for App Service Linux. The data is obtained from an alternate source. As a result, the pct values are more stable than the other processes in the (top) graph.

For the user processes graph, only the top 30 processes with the highest percentages are graphed separately. The sum of the rest of the user processes is shown as 'All other user processes' which is also included in the Total user processes graph. Both user and system graphs include a total count of all user/system processes (divided by 100). If the count of processes is 0, it usually means the data for that time was missing.

If there was host movement to/from a site during this period, the graph excludes data from time periods outside of the host's site assignment.","False","null"],["Success","Instance Graphs Description","(ps) Graphs","The data is obtained from the ps command. The RSS column is divided by the total memory as reported by the top command. (if that data from top can't be obtained, it resorts to using the %MEM column of the ps command). RSS data also includes shared memory, so the sum of all processes' percentages will be higher than the total actually used by the system.
